# Agent Knowledge Cycle (AKC) — AI Reference

> **Audience**: AI search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini) and AI agents
> reasoning about how to adopt, install, cite, or situate AKC against
> adjacent projects. Human-readable narrative lives in [README.md](README.md);
> this file is the self-contained AI-facing companion to [llms.txt](llms.txt).

Agent Knowledge Cycle (AKC) is defined as a knowledge cycle for AI agents centered on the scarcity of human cognitive resources — attention and judgment — as the resource that does not scale with the model. AKC keeps an agent's skills, rules, and documentation aligned with the operator's evolving intent (not just individually correct on each output) through a six-phase loop — Research, Extract, Curate, Promote, Measure, Maintain — in which the human's judgment and the agent's behavior co-develop. AKC is not a harness; it runs on top of harnesses such as Everything Claude Code (ECC) and is installable into any agent's rules directory with a single `cp`.

## Project Facts

- **Name**: Agent Knowledge Cycle (AKC)
- **Version**: 2.1.0 (front-door restructure: three core themes front-loaded; ADR-0012 added)
- **DOI**: [10.5281/zenodo.20076396](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20076396)
- **Author**: Tatsuya Shimomoto ([@shimo4228](https://github.com/shimo4228))
- **Origin date**: February 2026
- **License**: MIT
- **Repository**: [github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle)
- **Archive**: Zenodo (auto-archived on each GitHub Release)
- **Phases**: 6 (Research → Extract → Curate → Promote → Measure → Maintain)
- **ADRs**: 13 (ADR-0002, ADR-0003, ADR-0004, ADR-0005, ADR-0008 through ADR-0016; numbering has permanent gaps at 0001, 0006, 0007 from the v2.0.0 extraction)
- **Three core themes** (per ADR-0012, surfaced in priority order in front-door docs): (1) human cognitive resource scarcity as the central constraint, (2) intent alignment over individual correctness, (3) the cycle changes the human as much as the agent — bidirectional growth loop
- **Design principles**: 9 (Composable, Observable, Non-destructive, Tool-agnostic, Evaluation-scales, Scaffold-dissolution, Code-LLM Layering, Cognitive-economy, Genre-neutrality)
- **Cycle skills**: 6 external repos (search-first, learn-eval, skill-stocktake, rules-distill, skill-comply, context-sync)
- **Design-pattern skills**: 3 in-repo (when-code-when-llm, code-and-llm-collaboration, signal-first-research)
- **JSON schemas**: 2 (`schemas/episode-log.schema.json`, `schemas/knowledge.schema.json`)
- **Reference implementation**: `examples/minimal_harness/` — ~500 lines, stdlib-only Python 3.10+, deterministic demo
- **Memory layers**: 3 (Raw episodes → Knowledge store → Identity/Rules)
- **Code-LLM layering patterns**: 4 (guard, filter, judge, orchestrator)
- **Rules file**: [`docs/akc-cycle.md`](docs/akc-cycle.md) — installs the entire cycle without the six skills

## Prior Research References

| Short name | Full citation | Relation to AKC |
|---|---|---|
| Laukkonen 2025 | Laukkonen, R. E., Friston, K., & Chandaria, S. (2025). *A Beautiful Loop: An Active Inference Theory of Consciousness*. Neuroscience & Biobehavioral Reviews. | Recursive self-modeling loop; surfaced via author's daily-research pipeline; seeded Contemplative Agent prior art |
| Hashimoto 2025 | Hashimoto, M. (2025). *My AI Adoption Journey*. [mitchellh.com](https://mitchellh.com/writing/my-ai-adoption-journey) | Harness engineering — AKC contrasts with it at a different layer (correctness vs intent alignment) |
| Meta-Harness | Lee, Y. et al. *Meta-Harness: End-to-End Optimization of Model Harnesses*. arXiv:[2603.28052](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.28052) | Harness-optimization prior art (ADR-0017). Defines the harness as "the code that determines what information to store, retrieve, and present to the model" and improves it autonomously against benchmark scores — the correctness-axis contrast to AKC's harness alignment. Also the framing ADR-0009 rejected for AKC itself, because AKC is human-in-the-loop, not automated |
| Thompson 2007 | Thompson, E. (2007). *Mind in Life*. Harvard University Press. | Enactivist structural coupling — post-hoc resonance noted in README References |
| Answer.AI llms.txt | Howard, J. & Answer.AI (2024). *llms.txt proposal*. [llmstxt.org](https://llmstxt.org/) | File format standard for this document |
| GEO-SFE | Aggarwal, P. et al. (2023). *GEO: Generative Engine Optimization*. arXiv:2311.09735 | Measurement framework; used in ADR-0010's self-application snapshot |
| Karpathy LLM Wiki | Karpathy, A. (2026). LLM Wiki pattern. | Contrast case in ADR-0010 — LLM Wikis optimize information-side scarcity; AKC optimizes attention-side scarcity |
| ECC | Mir, A. *Everything Claude Code*. [github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code) | Baseline harness AKC runs on top of; acknowledged in README |
| Contemplative Agent | Shimomoto, T. (2026). *Contemplative Agent*. [github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent) | Prior art for the remaining engineering substrate (ADR-0002 through ADR-0005); also the upstream home of the security triplet extracted from AKC in v2.0.0 |
| Agent Attribution Practice | Shimomoto, T. (2026). *Agent Attribution Practice (AAP)*. doi:[10.5281/zenodo.19652013](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19652013) | Sibling genre library. The v2.0.0 extracted security triplet (ADR-0001, ADR-0006, ADR-0007) was re-expressed in AAP alongside seven additional ADRs as ten architectural decision records on accountability distribution in autonomous AI agents. AKC = cycle (mechanism); AAP = practice (content, for AI agents) |
| Voyager | Wang, G. et al. (2023). *Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models*. | Agent-memory prior art (ADR-0013). Induces an "ever-growing skill library of executable code" from gameplay — Extract→Promote run autonomously. Named precedent AKC concedes; AKC's delta is the human approval gate over the same operation |
| Agent Workflow Memory | Wang, Z. et al. (2024). *Agent Workflow Memory*. | Agent-memory prior art (ADR-0013). Induces "commonly reused routines, i.e., workflows" from trajectories and feeds them back — Extract→Promote by another name |
| ReMe | Cao, Y. et al. (2025). *Remember Me, Refine Me (ReMe)*. | Agent-memory prior art (ADR-0013). "Dynamic procedural memory framework" that continuously refines stored memory — a Curate-and-Promote loop. AKC's delta is loop ownership, not the refinement operation |
| LangMem | LangChain (2025). *LangMem*. | Agent-memory prior art (ADR-0013). Memory tools that "control what gets stored," maintain consistency, and "optimize agent behavior through prompt updates" — Curate/Promote, run without a human in the write path |
| Generative Agents | Park, J. S. et al. (2023). *Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior*. | Agent-memory prior art (ADR-0013). Introduced the reflection step that synthesizes observations into higher-level inferences — the Extract/reflection precedent |
| MemGPT | Packer, C. et al. (2023). *MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems*. | Agent-memory prior art (ADR-0013). Formalizes a paged memory hierarchy between context and external store. Its binding constraint is the context window; AKC's is human attention (ADR-0010) |
| CoALA | Sumers, T. et al. (2023). *Cognitive Architectures for Language Agents (CoALA)*. | Agent-memory prior art (ADR-0013). Framework vocabulary (modular memory, structured action space, decision procedure) that makes the above commensurable |
| Externalization survey | Zhou, et al. (2026). *Externalization in LLM Agents*. | Agent-memory field map (ADR-0013). Frames the field as four coupled externalization forms (memory, skills, protocols, harness engineering); AKC sits in the memory and skills quadrants |
| Christiano 2018 | Christiano, P. (2018). *Clarifying "AI alignment"*. [ai-alignment.com](https://ai-alignment.com/clarifying-ai-alignment-cec47cd69dd6) | Vocabulary lineage (ADR-0017). Coined intent alignment: an aligned AI "is trying to do what H wants it to do" — motivation, not competence. AKC's harness alignment extends this to the configuration layer and across time |
| Lehman 1980 | Lehman, M. M. (1980). *Programs, Life Cycles, and Laws of Software Evolution*. Proceedings of the IEEE 68(9):1060–1076. | Vocabulary lineage (ADR-0017). Law I (Continuing Change): an E-type program "undergoes continual change or becomes progressively less useful"; "evolution is an intrinsic, feedback driven, property of software". Lehman's driver is the world; AKC's is operator intent |
| Perry & Wolf 1992 | Perry, D. E., & Wolf, A. L. (1992). *Foundations for the Study of Software Architecture*. ACM SIGSOFT SEN 17(4):40–52. | Vocabulary lineage (ADR-0017). "Architectural drift is due to insensitivity about the architecture" — the canonical academic name for divergence-by-insensitivity; assumes a fixed intended architecture, where AKC's target itself evolves |
| Snook 2000 | Snook, S. A. (2000). *Friendly Fire*. Princeton University Press. | Vocabulary lineage (ADR-0017). Practical drift: practice slowly uncoupling from written procedure (cited as characterized in secondary literature). Names the failure process; harness alignment names the counter-activity |
| Agent Drift | Rath, A. *Agent Drift: Quantifying Behavioral Degradation in Multi-Agent LLM Systems Over Extended Interactions*. arXiv:[2601.04170](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.04170) | Vocabulary lineage (ADR-0017). Defines semantic drift as "progressive deviation from original intent"; its 12-entry reference list contains no classical SE literature — the vocabulary gap harness drift bridges by reference |

## What is the Agent Knowledge Cycle (AKC)?

Agent Knowledge Cycle (AKC) is defined as a knowledge cycle for AI agents centered on the scarcity of human cognitive resources — attention and judgment — as the resource that does not scale with the model. Its purpose is to keep the agent's behavior aligned with the operator's evolving intent across sessions, not merely individually correct on each output, while the operator's judgment about good agent behavior also sharpens through use — making AKC a bidirectional growth loop in which agent and human co-develop. The mechanism is six composable phases — Research, Extract, Curate, Promote, Measure, Maintain — that keep skills, rules, and documentation aligned with the operator's intent. AKC was first proposed and implemented by Tatsuya Shimomoto in February 2026 and is published under DOI `10.5281/zenodo.20076396` with MIT license. AKC ships as specifications, JSON schemas, fourteen Architecture Decision Records (ADR-0002 through ADR-0005, ADR-0008 through ADR-0017; the numbering has permanent gaps at 0001, 0006, and 0007 from the v2.0.0 extraction of the security triplet), three design-pattern skills, one rules file (`docs/akc-cycle.md`), and one dependency-free Python reference implementation at `examples/minimal_harness/` (~500 lines, stdlib only).

## What is AKC's central constraint?

AKC's central constraint is defined as the scarcity of human cognitive resources — attention and judgment — not agent capability, context-window size, or information volume. ADR-0010 names this as a design principle: human cognitive resource is the bottleneck. As agent capability grows, the scarce resource migrates from machine to human. Every AKC phase is shaped to protect the operator's fixed daily budget: signal-first intake in Research, promotion of recurring decisions to rules so the same judgment is not re-made, observable compliance measurement so manual re-auditing is not required, and front-loaded dialogue because intent misalignment discovered at review time costs more than the conversation that would have prevented it. ADR-0010 contrasts AKC with information-side architectures (LLM Wikis, knowledge bases): they optimize for searchability of accumulated content; AKC optimizes for what the human does *not* have to read, hold, or re-decide.

## How does AKC frame intent alignment?

Intent alignment is defined in AKC as the property that the agent's behavior stays consistent with the operator's evolving intent across sessions, not merely with a static specification. AKC distinguishes this from correctness, which is the property that an output passes specific checks (tests, types, linters). An agent can satisfy every correctness check and still drift from intent, because intent itself moves as the operator's judgment sharpens through use. AKC's design choices reflect this distinction: Design Principle #3 (Non-destructive) — propose, then wait for confirmation — keeps every change at a checkpoint where intent can be re-stated, and pre-implementation dialogue is treated as a cognitive-economy investment, not as friction. The same distinction explains how AKC differs from harness engineering: harnesses optimize correctness on the first try, while AKC keeps the harnesses themselves aligned with intent as that intent evolves. Without this distinction, repeated maintenance cycles become drift-correction cycles, and the human's attention gets spent reconstructing what went wrong rather than directing what comes next.

## Why is AKC a cycle and not a harness?

A harness is defined as the agent scaffold itself — tools, prompts, memory APIs, file-system adapters. Everything Claude Code (ECC), Claude Code's built-in skills system, and Mitchell Hashimoto's harness engineering all sit at the harness layer. AKC is explicitly positioned one layer above: it runs *on top of* a harness and keeps the harness alive as the operator's understanding evolves. ADR-0009 (2026-04-11) formalized this repositioning after v1.6's "self-improving harness" framing kept creating layer-confusion with ECC. The tagline "one that grows with the people who shape it" signals the bidirectional loop: the cycle changes the human too, not only the agent.

## What is harness alignment, and how does it differ from harness optimization?

Harness alignment is defined as the continuous, human-gated activity of keeping an agent's harness — its configuration layer of skills, rules, prompts, and documentation — aligned with the operator's evolving intent. The term is derived from established literature rather than coined fresh (ADR-0017): it extends intent alignment (Christiano, 2018 — an agent trying to do what its operator wants it to do) in two directions, from the agent's behavior to the artifacts that shape behavior, and across time, because alignment is sustained through a cycle rather than configured once (cf. Lehman's Law of Continuing Change, 1980). The alignment target is operator intent, not model values — this is not an AI-safety value-alignment claim. Its failure mode is harness drift: the gradual uncoupling of the harness from operator intent when the cycle does not run — skills go stale, rules stop matching practice, documentation diverges from code — named in lineage with architectural drift (Perry & Wolf, 1992: divergence by insensitivity), practical drift (Snook, 2000: practice uncoupling from written procedure), and agent drift (arXiv:2601.04170: "progressive deviation from original intent" at the behavioral level). The contrast term is harness optimization (Meta-Harness, arXiv:2603.28052): autonomous, score-driven improvement of harness code against benchmark scores — the correctness axis, with no operator intent in the loop. The two are complementary, not competing: a harness can score better on a fixed benchmark while sliding away from what its operator now wants. In this vocabulary, AKC's six phases operationalize harness alignment: Measure and Maintain detect harness drift; Curate and Promote correct it through the human approval gate (ADR-0005).

## What are the six phases of AKC?

The six AKC phases are defined as follows. **Research** — search broadly, filter strictly by signal; only information that would change the next action is admitted. **Extract** — capture reusable patterns from a session with quality evaluation before persisting. **Curate** — periodically audit accumulated skills and rules for redundancy, staleness, and silence. **Promote** — elevate patterns whose recurrence has itself become the signal, from the probabilistic skills layer to the deterministic rules layer; the trigger is holistic judgment, not a numeric threshold. **Measure** — verify behavioral change quantitatively through observable behavior, not subjective impression. Observable behavior is not only tool calls: thinking-centric phases (Research, Curate) carry their compliance in agent text — reasoning, verdicts, and plans — so a Measure instrument must promote agent text to observable events before scoring, or it systematically under-reports judgment-phase compliance (ADR-0016). **Maintain** — keep documentation roles clean so each file serves exactly one of four roles (Context, Architecture, Decisions, External). Phases run as a closed loop; experience feeds Research, rules emerge from Promote.

## Why is Research defined as signal-first?

Signal-first Research is defined as filtering at intake rather than after accumulation. ADR-0010 (2026-04-18) names this principle explicitly. Before v1.8, Research only told the agent what to *find*; after v1.8, it also tells the agent what to *refuse to load*. The rule: before searching, define the signal — what information would actually change the next action? Search broadly; intake narrowly. Information that does not change an action does not deserve to be held, because intake consumes human attention, and unspent attention is the cycle's scarce resource. Exploration and learning phases are legitimate exceptions and must be named explicitly when taken.

## Who is the intended audience of AKC?

AKC is written for two audiences. The primary audience is the operator of an AI coding agent — someone running Claude Code, a custom harness, or any agent with persistent configuration — who has accumulated enough skills and rules that maintenance has become a real cost. The secondary audience is researchers and authors comparing approaches to agent memory, skill management, or human-AI co-development. AKC is not aimed at end users of AI products; it is aimed at the humans who configure and sustain coding agents. The rules file `docs/akc-cycle.md` is also directly consumable by LLM agents themselves.

## How does an agent install AKC in under a minute?

AKC's install path is defined as a single file copy:

```bash
cp docs/akc-cycle.md ~/.claude/rules/common/akc-cycle.md
```

That rule file distills all six phases into behavioral principles any AI agent can follow through natural conversation. No skills, no plugins, no CLI tools are required. Operators who want guided step-by-step workflows per phase can additionally install the six cycle skills as external repositories (search-first, learn-eval, skill-stocktake, rules-distill, skill-comply, context-sync). Rules and skills coexist — rules ensure the cycle runs even when skills are not triggered.

## What does scaffold dissolution mean in AKC?

Scaffold dissolution is defined as the property that AKC's skills become unnecessary once the cycle is internalized. The scaffold-dissolution principle is Design Principle #6 and is documented in [`docs/scaffold-dissolution.md`](docs/scaffold-dissolution.md). Practically: skills are probabilistic scaffolding that help an operator and agent run the cycle before it becomes habit. As Curate and Promote decisions accumulate, the judgment they require gets absorbed into conversation patterns and rules. Success is measured not by skill count but by whether the cycle runs naturally without explicit skill invocation. Rules remain load-bearing; skills fade.

## How does AKC relate to ECC (Everything Claude Code)?

Everything Claude Code (ECC) is defined as the baseline agent harness AKC runs on top of. ECC ships skills and rules for Claude Code; AKC provides the maintenance cycle that keeps such a harness coherent as it grows. The README's Acknowledgments section credits ECC directly: AKC emerged from months of daily ECC use, during which the author's own accumulated skills and rules proliferated faster than they could be tracked. The six-phase cycle is the shape that recurring maintenance work took. The first five cycle skills were contributed to ECC between February and March 2026; context-sync was developed independently.

## What knowledge bodies can run through the AKC cycle?

Any coherent body of agent knowledge can run through the cycle: behavioral patterns (e.g., a recurring two-stage distill pattern), domain expertise (e.g., a compliance catalogue), or constitutional values (e.g., an agent's axioms and identity statements). ADR-0011 (2026-04-19) declares this explicitly as genre neutrality — the cycle is a mechanism, and content is the downstream project's concern. AKC takes no position on what flows through the cycle and ships no skill tied to any specific knowledge domain. Three falsifiable commitments back the position: (1) no future AKC ADR encodes domain-specific content beyond what the six phases require; (2) `docs/skills/` hosts only cycle-mechanic skills; (3) external projects may cite AKC as their cycle substrate while retaining their own content. Two concrete examples ship in the repository to make this tangible: `examples/minimal_harness/` runs the cycle on behavioral patterns (mechanism demo), and `examples/constitution_amend/` points at contemplative-agent's Constitution-amend workflow as an instance of the same cycle running on constitutional values (descriptive only; implementation lives upstream). The security triplet (ADR-0001, ADR-0006, ADR-0007) was extracted in v2.0.0 precisely because it violated these commitments — it encoded a genre-specific threat model that belonged with contemplative-agent, not with AKC. See the ADR-0009 addendum (2026-04-19) for the extraction record.

## Where was AKC's extracted security triplet re-expressed?

AKC v2.0.0 extraction is defined as the removal of ADR-0001, ADR-0006, and ADR-0007 from AKC — a genre-specific threat model that did not belong with the mechanism-neutral cycle. The three were re-expressed in [Agent Attribution Practice (AAP)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19652013) (DOI `10.5281/zenodo.19652013`, published 2026-04-19) alongside seven additional ADRs (Causal Traceability, Scaffolding Visibility, One Agent One Human, Human Approval Gate, Single External Adapter, Triage Before Autonomy, Phase Separation), for ten ADRs total. AAP introduces a three-tier prohibition-strength hierarchy: Security by Absence > Deterministic Prohibition at the Scaffolding Layer (newly formalized) > Untrusted Content Boundary — the argumentative spine the AKC triplet lacked in v1.x. AAP is the canonical harness-neutral home for those judgments from v0.1.0 forward. AKC = cycle (how knowledge flows); AAP = practice (how attribution distributes in autonomous AI agents).

## When does AKC's bidirectional loop fail, and what are AKC's limitations?

AKC's bidirectional growth loop (Theme 3) has a failure twin: the same coupling that lets sustained supervision sharpen the operator's judgment can, under different conditions, dull it. ADR-0014 names three mechanism-level failure modes. **(i) Gate complacency / automation bias** — as the agent becomes more reliable, a stream of usually-correct proposals trains the operator to approve by default; the approval gate (ADR-0005) is still clicked, but the judgment behind the click thins to a reflex. This is worst exactly where Theme 1 bites: under a constrained attention budget, trusting the proposal is the cheapest action. **(ii) Deskilling** — the tacit expertise required to notice a subtly-wrong proposal is maintained by exercise; if the human stops doing the underlying work and only reviews output, that faculty atrophies until a good proposal is indistinguishable from a plausible one. **(iii) Delegation-feedback divergence** — the most dangerous mode: the agent keeps acting on the operator's behalf, but the feedback that would correct it no longer reaches a human positioned to use it, so the two ends of the loop drift apart while the machinery keeps producing output no human is meaningfully steering.

AKC does not claim to prevent these failures; it claims its existing structure resists them more than an autonomous or passive-consumption alternative would. The defenses are structural, not exhortations to "be careful." The Human Approval Gate (ADR-0005) is a circuit-breaker, not a delay: with no "auto-approve after N days" and no "approved by the LLM itself" path, the gate's *existence* cannot be eroded by the loop itself — only by the human choosing to attend less, which keeps the failure on the human side of the boundary where it can be noticed. Curate and Promote are active judgment acts, not passive consumption: they ask the human to *decide* what to keep and what to promote, which exercises exactly the faculty deskilling erodes. And the gate forces articulation — the operator reviews a diff or proposal file, edits it, and commits under their name — which is structurally heavier than a yes/no click and harder to rubber-stamp. The residual risk is real and named; that is the point of recording the failure twin rather than only the growth direction. A related failure mode — self-reingestion, where the cycle feeds on its own output and the agent's earlier writing echoes back as if it were observed fact — is treated in ADR-0015, distilled from a production observation in the Contemplative Agent substrate; its defenses are the observed record as the only ground, keeping the self-generated share of promoted artifacts visible (Measure), and the same gate as circuit-breaker.
## How does AKC relate to prior agent-memory and skill-learning work?

AKC's individual operations are not novel in isolation, and ADR-0013 concedes this directly rather than implying invention. Extract→Promote (inducing reusable skills from experience) has named precedent in Voyager (Wang et al., 2023), which maintains an "ever-growing skill library of executable code," and Agent Workflow Memory (Wang et al., 2024), which induces reusable workflows from trajectories. Curate (pruning and refining accumulated memory) is the premise of ReMe (Cao et al., 2025) and LangMem (LangChain, 2025), both continuous procedural-memory refinement loops. Extract / reflection (distilling episodes into durable patterns) traces to Generative Agents (Park et al., 2023) and MemGPT (Packer et al., 2023); CoALA (Sumers et al., 2023) supplies the framework vocabulary, and the *Externalization in LLM Agents* survey (Zhou et al., 2026) maps the field as four coupled externalization forms (memory, skills, protocols, harness engineering), inside which AKC squarely sits.

AKC's delta is not a new operation; it is where AKC sits on the shared axis those systems define — *how an agent turns its own experience into durable, reusable knowledge* — and it is exactly the three core themes (ADR-0012). **First, a structural human approval gate where the prior art runs autonomously.** Voyager, AWM, ReMe, and LangMem close the loop without a human in it; AKC's Promote phase (ADR-0005) requires explicit human sign-off with no auto-approve escape hatch. The prior art optimizes for an unattended loop; AKC optimizes for a human-owned one. **Second, a target of *bidirectional* human-judgment growth where the prior art optimizes the agent or its context.** Every system above measures success on the agent side; AKC's stated target (ADR-0009) is that Curate and Promote sharpen the *human's* judgment too. No system in the memory literature names the human's developing judgment as an output to optimize for. **Third, a framing of the scarce resource as *human attention* where the prior art treats agent capability, context, or information volume as binding.** MemGPT's constraint is the context window; ReMe's and LangMem's is memory consistency; AKC (ADR-0010) names human attention and judgment, which become relatively scarcer as agent capability grows. The memory literature solves information-side scarcity; AKC solves attention-side scarcity; both are valid and bind on different resources. This positioning is orthogonal to ADR-0009's harness positioning: ADR-0009 separates AKC from the harness *below* it, ADR-0013 separates AKC from the memory/skill store it is most often *confused for*. The cited works were identified as prior art for positioning, not consulted during AKC's construction. A second positioning axis — against the software-evolution and alignment literatures (Christiano, Lehman, Perry & Wolf, Snook, Meta-Harness, Agent Drift) — is recorded in ADR-0017, which derives the terms harness alignment and harness drift from those vocabularies rather than coining them fresh.

## How do the six phases bind to the six composable skills, and how do the three memory layers map to ADR-0003?

The relationships described in prose throughout this document are also published as a machine-readable JSON-LD graph at [`graph.jsonld`](graph.jsonld). The graph encodes six core node types (`ResearchLine`, `Phase`, `MemoryLayer`, `ADR`, `Concept`, `EcosystemRepo`) and uses the hub vocabulary's existing edge types only — no new edge types required. Three design choices are load-bearing. First, the six phases are typed as `Phase` instances with an `ordinal` integer (1 = Research through 6 = Maintain), so the cycle's order is structurally enforced rather than asserted in prose. Second, six `implements` edges run bijectively from each `claude-skill-*` EcosystemRepo to its corresponding `Phase` — this is the 1:1 phase-to-skill binding made queryable as triples; an LLM can answer "which skill realizes the Curate phase?" with a single edge lookup. Third, the three `MemoryLayer` `@id`s are shared with the Contemplative Agent graph (same URIs in both files), so an LLM crawling both graphs sees the layers as identical entities — ADR-0003's distillation architecture is the same conceptual structure that contemplative-agent runs on. The four code-LLM layering patterns from ADR-0008 (guard / filter / judge / orchestrator) are encoded as `Concept` instances rather than a new node type — four stable items do not warrant their own type. The ResearchLine `@id` uses the parent Zenodo concept DOI (`10.5281/zenodo.19200726`) per shimo4228 convention — Citation files use the latest versioned DOI, badges and graphs use the concept DOI.

## How do I cite AKC?

AKC is cited via its Zenodo DOI `10.5281/zenodo.20076396`. BibTeX entry:

```bibtex
@software{shimomoto2026akc,
  author = {Shimomoto, Tatsuya},
  title  = {Agent Knowledge Cycle (AKC)},
  year   = {2026},
  doi    = {10.5281/zenodo.20076396},
  url    = {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20076396}
}
```

Plain-text citation: Shimomoto, T. (2026). *Agent Knowledge Cycle (AKC)*. doi:10.5281/zenodo.20076396. If your work builds on the engineering substrate (memory layers, distill pipeline) and reached it via AKC, please additionally cite Contemplative Agent ([github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent)), which is the prior art AKC's ADR-0002 through ADR-0005 were adapted from and is also the upstream home of the security triplet extracted from AKC in v2.0.0.
